audit_trail.notes: Two adversarial review cycles were completed. All three reviewers noted that the HTML source bundle supplied omitted all numbered equations (rendered as empty table cells), preventing direct algebraic verification; reviews were conducted from prose, equation labels, and structural arguments only. In cycle 2, gemini substantially revised its scores downward after adopting objections raised by codex (action non-uniqueness) and claude (Wald tensor reduction, HTML artefact). The two objections rated 'major' — Wald entropy non-uniqueness and Wald tensor reduction to 2D — both survived adversarial cross-review and were accepted by all reviewers by cycle 2. The 'contested' vs. 'promising' verdict disagreement between codex and the other two reviewers was not resolved; it reflects a genuine interpretive difference about whether identified justification gaps constitute potential errors or merely incomplete exposition of an otherwise sound framework.

general_reader_takeaway: This 7-page letter tries to give a single recipe for computing the thermodynamics — temperature, entropy, and energy — of a broad class of black holes that appear in quantum-gravity phenomenology, including so-called "regular" black holes that avoid a central singularity. The review board found the conceptual strategy promising and the worked example consistent, but identified two technical gaps — concerning whether the entropy formula is uniquely pinned down and whether a key dimensional-reduction step is fully justified — that are not resolved in the letter itself. A further caveat is that the foundational theorems the letter builds on are stated in concurrent companion preprints rather than proved here.
human_spotchecks: Read the PDF directly to verify all numbered equations (1)–(72), which were unrendered in the HTML bundle supplied to reviewers. In particular, confirm the explicit forms of the generating function Ω, the Wald entropy formula (eq. 46), the h_4 definition (eq. 34), and the Bardeen AdS example (eqs. 67–72).

Check whether the reconstruction formulas (eqs. 32–34) fix the integration constant in h_4 canonically and whether different valid choices produce different Wald entropies for the same black hole metric. If they do, assess what normalization convention resolves the ambiguity.

Verify that the D-dimensional Wald entropy tensor contraction (eqs. 40–44) is fully captured by the partial derivative of the 2D reduced Lagrangian for parent theories that include G_5-type or non-polynomial curvature-derivative invariants. This requires checking off-ansatz components of the Riemann variation against the 2D reduction.

Access the companion preprints Borissova (2026) arXiv:2603.06786 and Carballo-Rubio (2026) arXiv:2507.15920 to assess whether they contain complete, self-contained proofs of the reconstruction theorem, integrability condition, and Birkhoff theorem on which this letter's results depend.

Assess whether the principle of symmetric criticality, as stated in Palais (1979) and Fels & Torre (2002) for compact symmetry groups, applies without additional argument to compact hyperbolic horizon topologies H^{D-2}/Γ, where the relevant continuous isometry group SO(D-2,1) is non-compact and only the discrete cocompact lattice quotient is compact.

Check the Bardeen AdS example for correctness of topology and sign conventions, and confirm that the claimed regularity (absence of curvature singularity) holds globally for the metric function (eq. 67), not merely locally at the algebraic level.
public_summary: Borissova proposes a unified framework for black hole thermodynamics covering a wide class of D-dimensional static spacetimes with g_tt g_rr = −1 in Schwarzschild gauge, a condition satisfied by many phenomenological black holes motivated by quantum gravity. The strategy is to embed any such black hole as a vacuum solution of a generalized quasi-topological gravity by identifying a "generating function" Ω in an integrable 2D reduced theory. From Ω one can read off the thermodynamic mass, Hawking temperature, and — via Wald's Noether charge formalism applied to the 2D Lagrangian — the entropy, without needing to know the explicit form of the higher-dimensional action. A generalized first law including pressure-volume terms and couplings, along with a Smarr formula via Euler scaling, is developed and illustrated on the asymptotically AdS Bardeen regular black hole. The board rates the overarching strategy as coherent and timely for the growing literature on regular black holes. Two concerns survive cross-review: the Wald entropy formula rests on an integration constant that must be canonically fixed (a step the letter does not complete), and the claim that the D-dimensional Noether charge reduces to a 2D partial derivative is asserted without proof for the most general theory class claimed. Foundational inputs — the reconstruction theorem and a Birkhoff theorem for extended quasi-topological gravities — are imported from companion preprints and cannot be independently verified within this letter. Note: the HTML source supplied to reviewers omitted all numbered equations; this is a rendering artefact, not a flaw in the paper, but it prevented direct algebraic verification by the board.
reviewer_disagreements: Gemini (cycle 1) assigned technical soundness 8/10 and overall 7/10, notably higher than claude and codex (both 6/10 overall). After cross-review in cycle 2, gemini revised downward to technical soundness 5/10 and overall 6/10, converging substantially with the other reviewers. The initial divergence reflected gemini's relative acceptance of the Wald entropy reduction step; the convergence reflects adoption of the action non-uniqueness and Wald-tensor-reduction objections raised by codex.

Codex recommended a 'contested' verdict in both cycles, while claude and gemini both recommended 'promising'. Codex's more skeptical position rests on the view that the action-level ambiguity in the Wald entropy is not merely a gap in justification but a potential error in the result; claude and gemini treat it as a fixable gap that does not undermine the overall framework.

All three reviewers agreed that the HTML rendering failure (all numbered equations absent from the supplied bundle) is a review-process artefact rather than a paper flaw, and none counted it as a scientific objection against the paper.

strongest_objections.objection: The Wald entropy formula (eq. 46) is not uniquely determined by the generating function Ω alone. The relevant 2D Lagrangian coefficient h_4 is defined via an indefinite integral (eq. 34), and different choices of integration constant — corresponding to topological or boundary terms in the parent D-dimensional action — can shift the Wald entropy by a horizon-evaluated additive term while leaving the field equations and Ω unchanged. The letter does not argue that its specific reconstruction formulas (eqs. 32–34) fix this ambiguity uniquely, nor does it specify an entropy-normalization convention beyond the polynomial curvature examples cited in footnote 8.

The reduction of the full D-dimensional Iyer-Wald entropy tensor contraction to a partial derivative of the 2D reduced Lagrangian is asserted but not derived for the broad class of non-polynomial or curvature-derivative parent theories that the letter advertises. The argument rests on the claim that the 2D Lagrangian depends only linearly on the 2D Ricci scalar; this may not hold for parent theories whose D-dimensional Lagrangian involves curvature-derivative invariants, or when off-ansatz components of the Riemann variation contribute to the binormal contraction.

The foundational reconstruction theorem (any g_tt g_rr = -1 black hole can be recast as a quasi-topological gravity vacuum), the associated Birkhoff theorem, and the integrability condition are all stated without proof and are imported wholesale from concurrent companion preprints (Borissova 2026 arXiv:2603.06786; Borissova & Carballo-Rubio 2026a,b; Carballo-Rubio 2026 arXiv:2507.15920; Boyanov & Carballo-Rubio 2025 arXiv:2506.14875). The validity of the present letter's thermodynamic results is conditional on those papers being complete and correct.
strongest_objections.paper_location: 'Extended quasi-topological gravities' section; reconstruction paragraph before eq. (30); footnotes 5, 11

strongest_objections.objection: The temperature and entropy derivations implicitly require that the outer horizon is a simple (non-degenerate) zero of f(r), so that the surface gravity κ is non-zero and the Euclidean periodicity argument applies. This assumption is never stated explicitly, yet regular black holes — the primary target application — commonly admit extremal configurations where f'(r_+) → 0 and T → 0, and where the present formulas break down or require separate treatment.

The first law as derived in eq. (48) is valid only for one-parameter variations along a fixed-coupling branch with r_+ as the single thermodynamic variable. It is not labelled as such before the extended first law (eq. 50) is introduced, creating potential for misreading the scope of the initial result.

A likely typographical error appears in the Euclidean periodicity statement: the text appears to give the imaginary-time period as κ/2π whereas the correct inverse temperature is 2π/κ. The downstream formula T = f'(r_+)/4π appears correct, suggesting a wording rather than derivational failure, but the text as written is inconsistent.

strongest_supported_claims: The generating function Ω in the integrated equation of motion determines the thermodynamic mass via M = Ω(r_+) at the outer horizon; the GR/AdS limit correctly reproduces the ADM mass, providing a concrete calibration of the identification.

The conceptual architecture — treating any g_tt g_rr = -1 black hole as a 2D dilaton/Horndeski vacuum and inheriting Wald's formalism — is logically coherent and consistent with established results for polynomial curvature quasi-topological gravities.

The Bardeen AdS example is algebraically consistent: the displayed mass, temperature, and entropy are mutually compatible, and the entropy is claimed to reduce to the Schwarzschild-AdS Bekenstein-Hawking area law in the limit where the regularisation parameter vanishes.

The generalized first law and Smarr formula are structurally sound for generating functions that are homogeneous in the relevant dimensionful scales, following as a standard consequence of Euler's theorem for the assumed ansatz.
technical_takeaway: The paper argues that any D-dimensional static black hole satisfying g_tt g_rr = -1 in Schwarzschild gauge can be interpreted as a vacuum solution of an integrable 2D dilaton/Horndeski theory, with a generating function Ω encoding both the metric and the thermodynamics. The mass is identified as M = Ω(r_+) at the outer horizon, the Hawking temperature follows from differentiating the level curve Ω(r, f) = M, and the Wald entropy is claimed to equal S = −4π ∫ dr_+ ∂_f Ω evaluated at f = 0, derived by reducing the D-dimensional Noether charge to a partial derivative of the 2D Lagrangian. A generalized first law and Smarr formula follow for generating functions that are homogeneous in the relevant dimensionful scales. Two objections survived adversarial cross-review as potentially verdict-changing: (1) the entropy formula depends on an integration constant in the definition of the 2D Lagrangian coefficient h_4 (eq. 34), and different constants — corresponding to topological or boundary terms — can shift the Wald entropy without altering the field equations, so the formula is not uniquely fixed by the generating function Ω alone; (2) the reduction of the full D-dimensional Iyer-Wald entropy tensor to a partial derivative of the 2D reduced Lagrangian is asserted but not derived for the broad class of non-polynomial or curvature-derivative parent theories advertised. The reconstruction theorem, Birkhoff theorem, and integrability condition that underpin the framework are deferred to concurrent companion preprints.
